question: What is the total revenue from all orders?
sql: SELECT SUM(amount) AS total_revenue FROM orders;
schema: Table: orders | Columns: id, customer_id, amount, status, created_at

question: Show me the top 3 cities by number of customers.
sql: SELECT city, COUNT(id) AS customer_count FROM customers GROUP BY city ORDER BY customer_count DESC LIMIT 3;
schema: Table: customers | Columns: id, name, city, created_at

question: Which warehouse has the most total stock?
sql: SELECT warehouse, SUM(stock_qty) AS total_stock FROM inventory GROUP BY warehouse ORDER BY total_stock DESC LIMIT 1;
schema: Table: inventory | Columns: id, product_id, warehouse, stock_qty, last_restock

question: Show all open high-priority support tickets with customer names
sql: SELECT st.id, st.subject, st.priority, c.name FROM support_tickets st JOIN customers c ON st.customer_id = c.id WHERE st.priority = 'high' AND st.status = 'open';
schema: Table: support_tickets | Columns: id, customer_id, subject, priority, status... Table: customers | Columns: id, name...

question: What is the average salary per department?
sql: SELECT d.name, AVG(e.salary) AS avg_salary FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.department_id = d.id GROUP BY d.name;
schema: Table: employees | Columns: id, name, department_id, salary... Table: departments | Columns: id, name...

question: Show me the revenue from customers of Cairo
sql:
SELECT SUM(orders.amount) AS total_revenue
FROM orders
JOIN customers ON orders.customer_id = customers.id
WHERE customers.city = 'Cairo';
schema:
Table: customers | Columns: id, name, city, created_at
Table: orders | Columns: id, customer_id, amount, status, created_at
Table: support_tickets | Columns: id, customer_id, subject, priority, status, created_at, resolved_at
Table: products | Columns: id, name, category, price

question: List the names of all our departments along with their IDs
schema:
Table: departments | Columns: id, name, budget
Table: employees | Columns: id, name, department_id, role, salary, hire_date
Table: support_tickets | Columns: id, customer_id, subject, priority, status, created_at, resolved_at
Table: inventory | Columns: id, product_id, warehouse, stock_qty, last_restock

question: Identify trends in customer behavior from the sales data.
sql:
WITH customer_sales AS (
    SELECT 
        customers.id AS customer_id,
        customers.name AS customer_name,
        SUM(orders.amount) AS total_spent,
        COUNT(orders.id) AS number_of_orders
    FROM 
        customers
    JOIN 
        orders ON customers.id = orders.customer_id
    GROUP BY 
        customers.id, customers.name
)
SELECT 
    customer_name,
    total_spent,
    number_of_orders,
    AVG(total_spent) OVER () AS avg_total_spent,
    AVG(number_of_orders) OVER () AS avg_number_of_orders
FROM 
    customer_sales
ORDER BY 
    total_spent DESC;
schema:
Table: support_tickets | Columns: id, customer_id, subject, priority, status, created_at, resolved_at
Table: orders | Columns: id, customer_id, amount, status, created_at
Table: customers | Columns: id, name, city, created_at
Table: inventory | Columns: id, product_id, warehouse, stock_qty, last_restock
